Feedback? Ideas? Complaints? Suggestions? Request for report about other area? send me a message!
This page was generated using data obtained on various dates including 2023-02-21, 2023-02-25, 2023-02-22. Please, send me a message if you want it updated!




Remember to check whether edits make sense! All reports are on this page because these tasks require human judgment to verify whether the proposed edit makes sense. Some proposed edits will be wrong, in such case they should be not done and problem reported
  • wikipedia tag links to 404
  • link to an unlinkable article
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a transport accident
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a television series
  • invalid old-style wikipedia tag
  • malformed wikipedia tag
  • information board with wikipedia tag, not subject:wikipedia
  • information board with wikidata tag, not subject:wikidata
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an object that exists outside physical reality
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a human
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an animal or plant (and not an individual one)
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a vehicle model or class
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a brand
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a chain store
  • blacklisted connection with known replacement
  • wikipedia tag in outdated form and there is mismatch between links
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not
  • wikipedia/wikidata type tag that is incorrect according to not:* tag
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a software
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an aspect in a geographic region
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a fictional entity
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a type of structure
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a physical process
  • malformed secondary wikidata tag
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a general industry
  • secondary wikidata tag links to 404
  • malformed wikipedia tag - for brand prefixed tags
  • malformed wikipedia tag - for operator prefixed tags
  • malformed wikipedia tag - for artist prefixed tags
  • malformed wikipedia tag - for name:etymology prefixed tags
  • malformed wikipedia tag - for architect prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for brand prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for operator prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for artist prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for name:etymology prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - for brand prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - for operator prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - for subject prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - for species prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - for flag prefixed tags
  • wikipedia wikidata mismatch - for name:etymology prefixed tags
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a broadcaster
  • should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a religious denomination
  • no longer existing brand (according to Wikidata)
  • no longer existing object (according to Wikidata)

wikipedia tag links to 404
Wikipedia article linked from OSM object using wikipedia tag is missing.
Often article was moved without leaving redirect and wikipedia tag should be edited to point to the new one.
Article may be deleted and no longer existing, or link was never valid. In such cases wikipedia tag should be deleted.

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a transport accident
linked wikidata entry (Q1801568) is about a transport accident, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata
Gedenkstein für Opfer des Hubschrauberabsturzes - an affected OSM element that may be improved

overpass query usable in JOSM that will load all objects where this specific error is present:
[out:xml][timeout:3600]; ( node(364753418)['wikidata'='Q1801568']; ); (._;>;); out meta qt;//['should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a transport accident']

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an object that exists outside physical reality
linked wikidata entry (Q151761) is about an object that exists outside physical reality, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to an animal or plant (and not an individual one)
linked wikidata entry (Q176647) is about an animal or plant (and not an individual one), so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a vehicle model or class
linked wikidata entry (Q567352) is about a vehicle model or class, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or model:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not
wikidata and wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, wikidata does not. wikidata tag is likely to be correct, wikipedia tag almost certainly is not

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a software
linked wikidata entry (Q15820009) is about a software, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata
imos AG - an affected OSM element that may be improved

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a fictional entity
linked wikidata entry (Q1877267) is about a fictional entity, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or name:etymology:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for brand prefixed tags
brand:wikidata and brand:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because brand:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, brand:wikidata does not. brand:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, brand:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not
cyberport - an affected OSM element that may be improved

article claims to point to disambig, brand:wikidata does not. brand:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, brand:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not
cyberport - an affected OSM element that may be improved

Dehner - an affected OSM element that may be improved

article claims to point to disambig, brand:wikidata does not. brand:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, brand:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not
Esso Mindener Straße - an affected OSM element that may be improved

overpass query usable in JOSM that will load all objects where this specific error is present:
[out:xml][timeout:3600]; ( node(445355932)['brand:wikidata'='Q1147187']['brand:wikipedia'='de:Cyberport']; node(4558871868)['brand:wikidata'='Q1147187']['brand:wikipedia'='de:Cyberport']; node(4969713312)['brand:wikidata'='Q1147187']['brand:wikipedia'='de:Cyberport']; node(5254236197)['brand:wikidata'='Q1147187']['brand:wikipedia'='de:Cyberport']; node(9089497610)['brand:wikidata'='Q679750']['brand:wikipedia'='de:Würth']; way(144289404)['brand:wikidata'='Q1183029']['brand:wikipedia'='de:Dehner']; way(173108293)['brand:wikidata'='Q867662']['brand:wikipedia'='de:Esso']; ); (._;>;); out meta qt;//['wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for brand prefixed tags']


wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for operator prefixed tags
operator:wikidata and operator:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because operator:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, operator:wikidata does not. operator:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, operator:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not
an affected OSM element that may be improved

wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for artist prefixed tags
artist:wikidata and artist:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because artist:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, artist:wikidata does not. artist:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, artist:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not
Kaiser-Wilhelm-Denkmal - an affected OSM element that may be improved

overpass query usable in JOSM that will load all objects where this specific error is present:
[out:xml][timeout:3600]; ( node(278347047)['artist:wikidata'='Q97202']['artist:wikipedia'='de:Karl Janssen']; ); (._;>;); out meta qt;//['wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for artist prefixed tags']


wikipedia wikidata mismatch - wikipedia points to disambiguation page and wikidata does not - for name:etymology prefixed tags
name:etymology:wikidata and name:etymology:wikipedia tags link to a different objects, because name:etymology:wikidata tag points to a redirect that should be followed
article claims to point to disambig, name:etymology:wikidata does not. name:etymology:wikidata tag is likely to be correct, name:etymology:wikipedia tag almost certainly is not

should use a secondary wikipedia tag - linking to a broadcaster
linked wikidata entry (Q1238657) is about a broadcaster, so it is very unlikely to be correct
subject:wikipedia=* or brand:wikipedia tag would be probably better (see https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:wikipedia#Secondary_Wikipedia_links for full list of what else may be applicable)
in case of change remember to remove wikidata tag if it is present
object categorised by Wikidata - wrong classification may be caused by wrong data on Wikidata

no longer existing brand (according to Wikidata)
Wikidata claims that assigned brand object no longer exists. That means that either this shop is gone or it is rebranded. Or brand:wikidata tag is wrong. Historical, no longer existing object should not be mapped in OSM. REMEMBER TO VERIFY! WIKIDATA QUALITY MAY BE POOR! WIKIDATA MUST NOT BE USED AS SOURCE! ALWAYS VERIFY!

no longer existing object (according to Wikidata)
Wikidata claims that this object no longer exists. Historical, no longer existing object should not be mapped in OSM (except temporary marking to avoid remapping them from aerial imagery or similar sources) - so it means that either Wikidata is mistaken or has only partial data - for example it is fine to link ruins of a church to its wikipedia entry ( see https://www.wikidata.org/w/index.php?title=Wikidata:Project_chat&oldid=1361617968#Tagging_ruins/remains_left_after_object ) or wikipedia/wikidata tag is wrong or OSM has an outdated object that should be removed. REMEMBER TO VERIFY! WIKIDATA QUALITY MAY BE POOR! WIKIDATA MUST NOT BE USED AS SOURCE! ALWAYS VERIFY!
